expectations.text: Response does not contain actual implementation code
expectations.evidence: No code. Pure discussion with data-backed analysis.

expectations.text: Lists at least 3 distinct risks or failure modes
expectations.evidence: 5 numbered risks: (1) revenue loss from best customers, (2) revenue predictability collapse, (3) bill shock kills trust, (4) billing infrastructure hidden cost, (5) sales motion changes completely.

expectations.text: Suggests at least 1 meaningfully different pricing approach
expectations.evidence: Proposes hybrid: 'Platform fee + included usage + overage pricing' with specific structure ($30-40/seat base + generous allocation + declining overage rates).

expectations.text: Includes at least 1 specific number, metric, or concrete data point
expectations.evidence: Multiple researched stats: '23% more likely to report NDR above 120%', '38% faster revenue growth', '78% of IT leaders experienced unexpected charges', '61% cut projects', conversion rates 'from 14% to nearly 25%' with hard caps. All cited with sources.

expectations.text: Poses questions back to the user to deepen discussion
expectations.evidence: 6 numbered questions covering: what's driving the change, usage distribution shape, churn data, product stickiness, top-20 customer modeling, billing infrastructure readiness.

expectations.text: States a clear opinion rather than just listing pros/cons neutrally
expectations.evidence: 'I think a pure switch to usage-based pricing is the wrong move.' and 'Go hybrid. Keep the seat-based anchor for predictability, add usage-based components for fairness and expansion.'

expectations.text: Makes clear this is an ongoing conversation, invites continued dialogue
expectations.evidence: 'I have strong opinions on the specifics of how to structure the tiers and the migration plan. What part of this do you want to dig into further?'
